chemistry.reaction_name: Photo-selective catalytic reduction (photo-SCR) of nitric oxide

additives.name: H2O
additives.role: enhances formation of active palladium species

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Pd0 species formed from PdO reduction via palladium oxide hydrate, palladium nitrate, and palladium acetate intermediates

chemistry.scope_summary: Photo-SCR of NO using propane as reducing agent over PdO/TiO2 photocatalyst. Temperature range 30-120°C, C3H8/NO molar ratios 0.8-16.8, water concentrations 0.55-1.6 v%. High NO removal efficiency (>90%) achieved at 70-100°C. Water enhances formation of active Pd species but optimal C3H8/NO ratio and temperature required.

optimization_table.key_findings: Optimal temperature window 70-100°C for DeNOx efficiency; water presence inhibits NO2 formation but enhances overall DeNOx efficiency

figures.light_source_position: above reactor through quartz glass window
figures.key_information: photochemical reactor with heated bottom plate and top illumination through quartz glass window
figures.caption: Figure 1. Cross-section of the photoreactor.

Figure 4. Proposed mechanism of palladium compounds trans- formation during photo-selective catalytic reduction in the presence of water and propane. | Figure 5 shows the dependence of DeNO x  efficiency on C 3 H 8 / NO ratio, water and temperature. The sub-figures are arranged from left to right in the order of low, medium and high C 3 H 8 / NO ratio. The sub-figures on the top row rep- resent the results obtained under high water concentration, while those on the bottom row represent the results obtained under low water concentration. figures.trend: varies by C3H8/NO ratio and H2O content
figures.caption: Figure 5. Comparison of DeNO x  efficiencies under different conditions; - • - dry atmosphere, - ■ - in the presence of water.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: No batch process described in this paper
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous gas-phase processing with precise temperature control and real-time monitoring of NO removal efficiency
translation_logic.what_changed: Implementation of continuous flow photoreactor with controlled temperature and gas flow rates
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: UV light irradiation through quartz window, PdO/TiO2 photocatalyst
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing of toxic NO gas with real-time monitoring

figure_descriptions: Cross-section of photoreactor showing quartz window, heated channel, and gas flow path
Optimization plots showing temperature dependence of DeNOx efficiency under different conditions
Mechanistic scheme for palladium transformation cycle during photo-SCR

notes: This is a gas-phase photocatalytic process for NO reduction, not a typical organic synthesis. No batch baseline comparison available. Temperature optimization clearly shown in Figure 5 with optimal range 70-100°C. Reactor design well-documented in Figure 1.
